title: Compliance — Research Security
content:
Morgan State University's Research Security Program fosters a secure and ethical research environment, with particular focus on federally sponsored research. The program protects research integrity while ensuring compliance with federal regulations across four core domains: (1) Cybersecurity — safeguarding sensitive research data, systems, and networks against cyber threats, including guidance on data protection and breach prevention; (2) Export Controls — ensuring compliance with U.S. export-control laws that govern the sharing of sensitive technologies, data, and research results with international partners; (3) Conflict of Interest — maintaining transparency through policies and procedures for identifying, disclosing, and managing potential conflicts of interest in federally funded research; (4) Foreign Travel Security — providing researchers with strategies to protect themselves and their work when traveling abroad, including guidelines for handling sensitive data internationally. The program is anchored to the federal NSPM-33 framework (National Security Presidential Memorandum 33 on United States Government-Supported Research and Development National Security Policy), which the ORA site lists as a dedicated resource topic. A Research Compliance and Security Training component is offered, with additional resources available through the Research Security Program Committee. General inquiries go to ask.ora@morgan.edu or 443-885-4044; the Office of Research Administration is at Tyler Hall, Student Service Building, Suite 304, Baltimore, MD 21251.

=== PLAYWRIGHT RE-VERIFICATION 2026-05-14 — Subpage Map ===
The Research Security parent page links out to 5 subpages via the ORA side navigation:
1. Information Technology Resources — /research-security/information-technology-resources — exposes 4 cybersecurity standards/procedures (Information Security Incident Response, Information Security Risk Management, Data Encryption, Data Classification). Aligns with NIST SP 800-37, NIST SP 800-53, ISO 27005, ISO 31000. See compliance_research_security_information_technology.
2. Research Compliance and Security Training — /research-security/research-compliance-and-security-training — lists 3 approved training providers (CITI Program, NSF SECURE Center, NSF Research Security Training Modules). Certificate retention required. See compliance_research_security_training (compliance angle) and trainings_compliance_security (catalog angle).
3. Technology Control Plan (TCP) — /research-security/technology-control-plan-(tcp) — STATUS: DocuSign template 'coming soon' as of 2026-05-14. Page has overview text only, no live form. See compliance_research_security_technology_control_plan.
4. Research Security Program Committee — /research-security/research-security-program-committee — STATUS: roster not yet published. Page has one descriptive paragraph only — no member names, chair name, charter, or meeting cadence. Contact ORA for current committee information. See compliance_research_security_program_committee.
5. NSPM-33 Overview — /research-security/nspm-33-overview — explains the $50M annual federal funding threshold trigger and lists NSF, NIH, DOE, DOD as the four agencies that built compliant training (delivered via CITI Programs). See compliance_research_security_nspm_33.

=== Page-completeness summary ===
Fully populated subpages: Information Technology Resources, Research Compliance and Security Training. Stub subpages awaiting content: Technology Control Plan (DocuSign pending), Research Security Program Committee (roster pending). Brief but complete: NSPM-33 Overview.
